name: Roxanne Wolf

status.source: Loses her eyes in Security Breach; survives and aids Cassie in the RUIN DLC

notes: The Glamrock era's fox-role analog is a wolf — there is no Glamrock Foxy.
Her insecurity-driven personality and bond with Cassie in RUIN made her a fan favorite.

name: Montgomery Gator

status.source: Can be shattered in Security Breach; found wrecked in the RUIN DLC

notes: Replaced Glamrock Bonnie as the band's bassist; his rivalry with Bonnie fuels the theory that he caused Bonnie's destruction (tracked as theory).
Verify the exact form of his RUIN appearance on the wiki.

name: Music Man
notes: Spider-like keyboardist that reacts to noise; debuted in Ultimate Custom Night.
Miniature Music Men roam the Mega Pizzaplex in Security Breach.

ucnVoiceLine.line: A song was requested of me, and now I sing it!
ucnVoiceLine.source: fnaflore.com audio transcripts, cross-referenced with the UCN voice cast credits

name: DJ Music Man
notes: Giant arena-sized version of Music Man running Fazcade's DJ booth.
Verify whether he appears in the RUIN DLC before adding sb-ruin.

name: Sun (Daycare Attendant)
notes: The Daycare Attendant's lights-on persona: hyperactive and safety-obsessed.
Sun and Moon are the same physical unit; cataloged as two identities following the Vanessa/Vanny precedent.

name: Moon (Daycare Attendant)
notes: The Daycare Attendant's lights-off persona: a hostile 'naptime enforcer'.
Same physical unit as Sun; the persona switches with the daycare lights.

name: Eclipse (Daycare Attendant)
notes: Balanced third persona combining Sun and Moon.
Verify its exact debut (RUIN vs Help Wanted 2) and role in each on the wiki.

name: Glamrock Endo
notes: Bare Glamrock endoskeletons that only move when unobserved, Weeping Angel-style; modeled as a single entry representing the group.

name: S.T.A.F.F. Bot
notes: The Mega Pizzaplex's utility robots (Map Bots, Security Bots, janitors, cleaners); modeled as a single entry representing the group.
Notable named units (e.g. the Comedy Bot) can be split into their own entries later if needed.

name: The Mimic
color: Bare metal with green paint traces
notes: Learning endoskeleton that copies behavior — including violence; the Steel Wool era's central antagonist.
Literary origin in 'The Mimic' story and the Tales from the Pizzaplex epilogues (see tftp-06-nexie in media); map the remaining epilogue volumes during a dedicated Tales sweep.
Secret of the Mimic explores its 1979 origin with creator Edwin Murray; verify details before expanding.
Theorized to be behind Glitchtrap/Burntrap instead of Afton; tracked as theory in those entries.

name: M.X.E.S.
notes: Security AI visible only through the V.A.N.N.I. mask's AR layer in RUIN; acts as the antagonist while actually guarding the Mimic's containment.
Its name spells 'MXES', community-read as 'Mixes'; interpretations vary — keep to confirmed facts.
